heading: Powers of officers and employees of Forest Service
label: 559c Powers of officers and employees of Forest Service
notes: Amendments1988—, in introductory provision substituted “1,000 special agents and law enforcement officers” for “500 officers and employees”, and struck out “within the boundaries of the National Forest System” after “shall have authority”.Pub. L. 100–690, § 6254(b)(1)Par. (2). , inserted “, within the exterior boundaries of the National Forest System,” after “conduct” and “and to conduct such investigations and enforcement of such laws outside the exterior boundaries of the National Forest System for offenses committed within the National Forest System or which affect the administration of the National Forest System (including the pursuit of persons suspected of such offenses who flee the National Forest System to avoid arrest)” before semicolon at end.Pub. L. 100–690, § 6254(b)(2)Par. (3). , inserted “, for offenses committed within the National Forest System or which affect the administration of the National Forest System;” before semicolon at end.Pub. L. 100–690, § 6254(b)(3)
amendments: Amendments1988—, in introductory provision substituted “1,000 special agents and law enforcement officers” for “500 officers and employees”, and struck out “within the boundaries of the National Forest System” after “shall have authority”.Pub. L. 100–690, § 6254(b)(1)Par. (2). , inserted “, within the exterior boundaries of the National Forest System,” after “conduct” and “and to conduct such investigations and enforcement of such laws outside the exterior boundaries of the National Forest System for offenses committed within the National Forest System or which affect the administration of the National Forest System (including the pursuit of persons suspected of such offenses who flee the National Forest System to avoid arrest)” before semicolon at end.Pub. children.text: conduct, within the exterior boundaries of the National Forest System, investigations of violations of and enforce and other criminal violations relating to marijuana and other controlled substances that are manufactured, distributed, or dispensed on National Forest System lands and to conduct such investigations and enforcement of such laws outside the exterior boundaries of the National Forest System for offenses committed within the National Forest System or which affect the administration of the National Forest System (including the pursuit of persons suspected of such offenses who flee the National Forest System to avoid arrest);section 841 of title 21

make arrests with a warrant or process for misdemeanor violations, or without a warrant or process for violations of such misdemeanors that any such officer or employee has probable cause to believe are being committed in his presence or view, or for a felony with a warrant or without a warrant if he has probable cause to believe that the person to be arrested has committed or is committing such felony, for offenses committed within the National Forest System or which affect the administration of the National Forest System;;1So in original.1
serve warrants and other process issued by a court or officer of competent jurisdiction;
search with or without warrant or process any person, place, or conveyance according to Federal law or rule of law; and
seize with or without warrant or process any evidentiary item according to Federal law or rule of law.
